items.title: It's back to work for Dr M [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former Prime Minister Tun Dr Mahathir Mohamad has returned to his office at Yayasan Kepimpinan Perdana in Putrajaya, nearly three months after sustaining a hip fracture in a fall at his home. Salute," wrote user Allison Por Li Fang. Keep working."

items.title: Ramanan denies he is 'Mr R', challenges Victor Chin to name him
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: PKR vice-president Datuk Seri R. Ramanan has denied allegations linking him to controversial businessman Victor Chin and the alleged "corporate mafia". "Later today, my division chiefs will be lodging police reports against Chin," he said, adding that it is most apparent Chin is blaming enforcement agencies because he wants asylum in the United Kingdom. he said.

items.title: Bau police issue two summonses to driver caught in viral dashcam clip for dangerous driving
items.summary: KUCHING, March 25 — A driver was issued two traffic summonses after being caught on a viral dashcam video driving dangerously along Jalan Bau-Kuching near Kampung Keranji, Bau on Sunday. Bau police chief Supt Mohd Haide A. Rahman said the incident, which occurred at about 5.34pm, involved a Perodua Kancil. Mohd Haide said the driver admitted to driving while drowsy after attending a religious event in Kampung Blimbing, Bau, and was travelling from Bau towards Kuching.

items.title: CAP: Make plastic-free water bottles compulsory for students amid heatwave
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Consumers' Association of Penang (CAP) has urged the Education Ministry to make it compulsory for schoolchildren to bring plastic-free water bottles to school to cope with the current hot weather. Its education officer, N.V. Subbarow, said this measure was necessary to ensure students remain hydrated and protected. Subbarow said students should be encouraged to drink more water during the heat spell, describing plain water as the best remedy against heat-related risks.

items.title: Home Minister says ‘corporate mafia’ probe ongoing, being carried out step by step
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — Investigations into allegations of a “corporate mafia” are being conducted methodically, one step at a time, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said today. Speaking at a press conference after the 219th Police Day celebrations at the Police Training Centre (Pulapol) here, Saifuddin said an investigation paper has been opened, with authorities first working to establish the direction of the probe before identifying the modus operandi and individuals involved. “Let us proceed step by step.

items.title: Ex-Sabah minister Peter Anthony released on parole after forgery conviction
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former Sabah infrastructure minister Datuk Peter Anthony has been released on parole after serving part of a three-year jail sentence for a forgery conviction. Peter later filed a revision application challenging the appellate court's decision, which had affirmed the sentence imposed by the Kuala Lumpur Sessions Court. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Religious affairs minister warns Muslims against normalising tattoos, stresses need for expert guidance
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 25 — Muslims have been advised to uphold religious boundaries and personal dignity, and to avoid practices that contravene Islamic law, such as tattooing, which should not be normalised in society. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan, in a statement today, reminded Muslims not to emulate actions that violate Islamic law. “Muslims must always safeguard religious limits and personal dignity, including refraining from acts that contradict Islamic teachings, such as tattooing, which should not be normalised.

items.title: Former Sabah minister Peter Anthony granted parole, returns home after serving part of cheating sentence
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 25 — Former Sabah state minister Datuk Peter Anthony, who is about a year into serving a three-year sentence for a cheating case at Kajang Prison, has reportedly been released on parole and returned home in the interior of Sabah. Deputy president of Parti Kesejahteraan Demokratik Masyarakat (KDM), Datuk Wetrom Bahanda, confirmed Peter’s release but declined to provide further details. Sabah police commissioner Datuk Jauteh Dikon said parole matters fall under the Prison Department and declined to elaborate.

items.title: Police call for tougher laws, upgrades after attacks on stations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have called for stronger laws and improved infrastructure at critical facilities following a series of attacks on police stations in recent years. Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said incidents such as the fatal shooting of an officer in Pinggiran, Subang in 2017 and the 2024 Ulu Tiram attack, which left two policemen dead, underscored the growing boldness of those challenging law enforcement. It is also an insult to enforcement institutions," he said in his Police Day address.

items.title: Tengku Zafrul: Asean chip race intensifies, Malaysia firmly in the mix
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Southeast Asia's semiconductor race is intensifying, with Malaysia firmly in the mix, said Malaysian Investment Development Authority chairman Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Abdul Aziz. He said Malaysia remains among Asean's key semiconductor players, backed by its long-standing strength in assembly, test and packaging, which accounts for about seven per cent of global capacity. We are seeing increasing movement into higher-value segments," Tengku Zafrul said in a LinkedIn post.

items.title: PKR will not shield any of its members - Fahmi
items.summary: Its information chief, Datuk Fahmi Fadzil, said this aligns with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's message that no group will be shielded from accountability for misconduct. Fahmi said PKR is ready to cooperate with the authorities following allegations involving a party member in the misappropriation of funds worth RM10 million. "The PKR leadership has so far been informed about news reports containing statements and allegations from the individual concerned.

items.title: MetMalaysia: Inter‑monsoon phase to bring severe weather nationwide, with thunderstorms and possible hail
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — The Monsoon Transition Phase is expected to begin this Sunday (March 29) and continue until May, leading to thunderstorms and heavy rains accompanied by strong winds in most areas in a short spell. Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) director-general Dr Mohd Hisham Mohd Anip said the areas expected to be affected are the west and interior of the Peninsular Malaysia, south and central Sarawak and the western part of Sabah. He said the phase would continue until May, marking the end of the 2025/2026 Northeast Monsoon which began on Nov 13 last year.

items.title: Govt to chart best- to worst-case risks in six-month outlook
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Cabinet has directed the Home Ministry and police to prepare a comprehensive analysis of geopolitical, economic, and security risks. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the study would include scenario planning ranging from best- to worst-case outcomes. "It will cover geopolitical, economic, and security scenarios, including what may happen if current global developments continue over the next six months," he told reporters after the national Police Day celebrations today.

items.title: Rafizi ordered to file defence in RM10mil defamation suit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Datuk Seri Mohd Rafizi Ramli has been ordered by the High Court to file his defence in a RM10 million defamation suit filed against him by social media influencer Ariz Ramli, better known as Caprice. Also in the proceedings was Rafizi's lawyer, Edmund Choi. "The court has ordered Rafizi to file his defence on or before April 9.

items.title: Saifuddin: Enforcement must be paired with engagement on 3R issues
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Firm enforcement must be complemented by engagement to manage sensitive issues involving race, religion, and royalty, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said. He said recent incidents — including religious disputes and statements that offended certain communities — highlighted the risks such issues pose. "Once such issues are triggered, they are difficult to control and can have harmful effects on society," he told reporters after the national Police Day celebrations today.

items.title: Drought disaster declaration lifted in Tawau, water supply in Sebatik still monitored
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 25 — Authorities have lifted the drought disaster declaration in Tawau following improved conditions, although water supply issues in Pulau Sebatik remain under close watch. In a statement, Tawau District Disaster Management Committee (JPBD) chairman Joseph Pang said the decision was made after recent assessments showed the situation no longer meets the criteria for a drought disaster. However, he noted that water supply shortages in Pulau Sebatik are still being closely monitored by authorities.

items.title: PM joins top leadership at 219th Police Day celebration at Pulapol, launches children’s crime‑awareness book
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today attended the 219th Police Day celebration at the Police Training Centre (PULAPOL) in Kuala Lumpur. He arrived at about 2 pm and was received by Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail, his deputy Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay and Bukit Aman police directors. Also present were Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, Deputy Home Minister Datuk Seri Dr Shamsul Anuar Nasarah, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil and Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar.

items.title: Saifuddin: Cops must stay 'steps ahead' as crime grows more complex
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police must remain "two to three steps ahead" of evolving crime and security threats, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said. He warned that the security landscape is becoming increasingly complex, citing cases where police officers themselves have been targeted. Investigations have uncovered structured criminal networks with multi-layered hierarchies, involving youths as young as 17 or 18 recruited and directed by older operatives, some based overseas.

items.title: Anwar warns Malaysia cannot take national security for granted amid global tensions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today warned that escalating global tensions, including the ongoing conflict involving Iran, should serve as a reminder that Malaysia cannot afford to take its national security for granted. Speaking at the 219th Police Day celebrations at the Police Training Centre (Pulapol) here, Anwar said the situation was complex and required careful understanding, particularly by the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM), given the potential security implications arising from global instability. We must not view national security issues lightly in times of such turmoil,” he said.

items.title: Govt to roll out proactive interventions to safeguard economic resilience, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — The government will immediately implement several proactive and thorough early intervention measures to maintain the country’s economic resilience amid the conflict in West Asia. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said this was among the matters decided when chairing the National Economic Action Council (MTEN) meeting today to examine the latest geopolitical developments, global economic pressures and security implications following the conflict. Anwar, who is also the Finance Minister, said the government would continue to closely monitor the conflict situation in West Asia, with information channelled directly to the public.

items.title: Saifuddin: Authorities know Victor Chin's whereabouts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Authorities know the whereabouts of businessman Victor Chin, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said. Saifuddin said police would first determine the direction of the investigation, identify the modus operandi and then call in those involved. He added that authorities would proceed methodically and would not rush into measures such as travel restrictions or passport seizures.

items.title: Malaysia targets top 10 in Global Peace Index within five years
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is aiming to break into the top 10 of the Global Peace Index (GPI) within five years, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said. He said Malaysia is currently ranked 13th out of 165 countries, placing it among the top three most peaceful nations in the Asia-Pacific, behind Singapore and New Zealand, and second in Asean. MORE TO COME © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Differing analyst views shape Sunway's IJM Corp takeover offer
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Research firms are largely supportive of Sunway Bhd's takeover offer for IJM Corp Bhd, but significant differences in valuation remain among independent analysts. Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd, which maintains an "Accept" recommendation, said the offer provides an opportunity to exit at a premium in a challenging market environment. MBSB Research and TA Securities also view the offer as fair, while RHB Research maintains a "Buy" call and CIMB Securities Sdn Bhd places a higher valuation of RM3.50 per share.

items.title: MIC stays with Barisan Nasional - Saravanan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: MIC will remain in Barisan Nasional, deputy president Datuk Seri M. Saravanan said at a press conference at the party's headquarters today. MORE TO COME © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Anwar tasks IGP, Home Minister with new security strategies amid global uncertainty
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called for new strategies to safeguard national security, warning that complacency could leave the country vulnerable amid growing global uncertainty. Speaking at the 219th Police Day celebrations today, Anwar urged Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail and Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail to formulate fresh approaches to ensure security remains under control. Anwar warned that the greatest mistake nations make is becoming too comfortable and assuming peace is guaranteed.

items.title: Shoplot owner ordered to pay TNB RM26,000 over tampered electricity meter
items.summary: KLANG, March 25 — A sessions court here has ordered shoplot owner Choo Lee Chen to pay Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) RM26,146.70 for an “undercharged” electricity bill, according to Free Malaysia Today (FMT). According to the report, TNB filed the suit in 2024 over alleged irregular electricity usage at Choo’s shoplot in Kota Bayu Emas Pendamar, Port Klang, claiming it was linked to Bitcoin mining. Sessions Court judge Noorhisham Jaafar clarified the calculation and held Choo liable as the registered consumer, saying she had a contractual relationship with TNB for electricity supply.

items.title: Malaysia steps up energy security efforts amid Strait of Hormuz risks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim chaired the third 2026 National Economic Action Council meeting to review geopolitical developments, global economic pressures and security implications following the Middle East conflict. He said the government will implement early, proactive and firm measures immediately to ensure economic resilience. Anwar said the government is stepping up efforts to secure energy supply, including diversifying sources and exploring alternative routes to reduce reliance on high-risk routes such as the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Who is Mr R? PKR’s Ramanan dares Victor Chin to name MP given RM9.5m
items.summary: SUNGAI BULOH, March 25 — PKR vice-president Datuk Seri R. Ramanan today dismissed insinuations that he is the lawmaker alleged to have received RM9 million from businessman Victor Chin, who is implicated in the so-called “Corporate Mafia” controversy. Ramanan challenged Chin to reveal the identity of “Mr. R”, while also taking aim at former party deputy Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli, suggesting he was trying to capitalise on Chin’s “sensational” allegations to attack the party.

items.title: Saravanan says MIC staying with BN for now, will study Indian sentiment on PAS-led Perikatan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — MIC will remain in Barisan Nasional (BN) for now, its deputy president Datuk Seri M. Saravanan said, but stopped short of ruling out a future move to Perikatan Nasional (PN), according to Free Malaysia Today (FMT). Saravanan said recent developments within PN require MIC to reassess its position, particularly following changes in the coalition’s leadership. “PN has undergone some changes.

items.title: OE EduGroup chairman sets MBR entrepreneurship participation record
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: OE EduGroup chairman Datuk Wira Dr Calvin Khiu has been recognised by the Malaysia Book of Records (MBR) for attracting the highest number of participants in an entrepreneurship programme. Khiu earned the title "Most Attendees in an Entrepreneurship Programme" after his programme recorded over one million participants across online and physical platforms. MBR said the achievement highlights the programme's large-scale impact in advancing practical entrepreneurship learning nationwide.

items.title: 'It wasn't me': Rafizi denies RM9.5m claim
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former economy minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli has denied allegations that he received RM9.5 million from businessman Victor Chin, amid mounting speculation online. In a social media post, Rafizi, who is also Pandan member of parliament, dismissed the claim in a light-hearted tone. In a separate post, Rafizi said the issue raised more serious concerns, particularly over how authorities respond to such allegations.

items.title: Malaysia resilient as fertiliser crunch hits Southeast Asia crops
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The risk of reduced application rates for 2026/2027 grain crops across Southeast Asia is increasing, particularly as key application windows approach, posing downside risks to production, according to BMI, a unit of Fitch Solutions. In a statement today, it said Malaysia is among the most insulated from nitrogenous fertiliser supply constraints, given its strong domestic production capacity, which should help limit downside risks to application rates, along with Indonesia and Vietnam. Beyond nitrogenous fertilisers, it expects South East Asia to face heightened risks from the phosphatic fertiliser supply chain.

items.title: Malaysia ranked 13th safest globally, second in Asean - IGP
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia has climbed to 13th place in the Global Peace Index 2025, reflecting improved security in the country, Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said today. Speaking at the 219th Police Day celebration at the Police Training Centre here today, he said the police would continue strengthening their capabilities to meet growing public expectations and address increasingly complex security challenges. Also present was Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil and Deputy Inspector General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay.

items.title: BMI sees oil at US$66 in Q2
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Oil prices are expected to fall to US$66 per barrel in the second quarter of 2026 (Q2), following a stronger performance of US$78 per barrel in the first quarter (Q1), before gradually recovering towards year-end, according to BMI. The research unit of Fitch Solutions said its forecast remains unchanged, with Brent crude projected at an annual average of US$70 per barrel for 2026. "However, our country risk team notes rising risk of a longer-duration war, assigning a 35 per cent probability to their 'extend-to-end' scenario (lasting up to eight weeks) and a 25 per cent probability to their 'extend-to-escalate' scenario (lasting multiple months).

items.title: Iran fires missile salvo after Trump signals progress in talks
items.summary: TEHRAN: Iran launched a new missile salvo across the Middle East on Wednesday, after US President Donald Trump's signals that he was eager to talk with Teheran steadied global markets. Trump, whose daily statements have swung wildly from threatening to conciliatory, said Washington was "in negotiations right now" with Teheran. The US leader did not explain further but said it related to the Strait of Hormuz, which Iran has largely blockaded in retaliation for US and Israeli strikes, spiking global energy prices.

items.title: Rafizi says ‘it’s not me’ amid RM9.5m PKR MP claim, jokes only Aidilfitri weight gained in Instagram post
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — Pandan MP Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli has denied allegations linking him to claims that a PKR MP received RM9.5 million to resolve a corporate mafia-related issue, saying “it’s not me” in a brief Instagram post. The response comes amid growing controversy following allegations by businessman Victor Chin Boon Loong, who in a 40-page document detailed a chronology of events claiming a PKR MP had demanded RM10 million to settle a corporate mafia-related matter. The corporate mafia allegations centre on claims that senior officials from the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) orchestrated intimidation campaigns and pressured executives and shareholders of listed companies to resign or divest their stakes at discounted prices to connected businessmen.

items.title: Population growth to drive 15pct rise in housing transactions: IQI
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's residential property transactions are projected to increase by 15 per cent over the next five years, driven primarily by population growth, according to IQI co-founder and group chief executive officer Kashif Ansari. He said population expansion will lead to the formation of about 582,000 new households in that period, providing a strong underlying driver for housing demand nationwide. Malaysia's population is expected to grow by 2.2 million people, from 34.3 million currently to 36.5 million by 2030.

items.title: Umno Supreme Council to decide on former leaders' applications to return
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Umno Supreme Council will make the final decision on applications and appeals from former leaders and political figures seeking to rejoin the party, after a vetting process by the Rumah Bangsa Unification Committee. Among those reported to have submitted appeals or applications to return are former vice-president Datuk Seri Hishammuddin Hussein and former Umno youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin. Others include former Jempol Umno chief Datuk Seri Mohd Salim Mohd Sharif and former Tebrau Umno chief Datuk Maulizan Bujang.

items.title: Fire losses exceed RM250mil since January
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The Fire and Rescue Department has recorded fire-related losses exceeding RM250 million nationwide since January. He said the losses were recorded from 5,869 fire cases nationwide. "Since January this year, we have recorded 5,869 fire cases, with Johor topping the list at 1,077 cases, followed by Kedah (984) and Selangor (906).

items.title: Melaka police arrest four, cripple Harraz Gang behind spate of vehicle thefts and burglaries
items.summary: MELAKA, March 25 — Melaka police have successfully dismantled an organised crime group known as the “Harraz Gang”, believed to be the mastermind behind a string of vehicle thefts, housebreaking and robberies in the Melaka Tengah district since the beginning of this year. Melaka Tengah district police chief, Assistant Commissioner Christopher Patit said the success followed the arrest of four local men in a special operation, ‘Op Lejang Melaka’ carried out by the D4 (Intelligence/Operations) Division of the Criminal Investigation Department (CID) in Permatang Pasir, Alai, on March 19. Christopher said the case is being investigated under Section 379A of the Penal Code for vehicle theft, Section 457 of the Penal Code for housebreaking and Section 392 of the Penal Code for robbery.

items.title: Couple to be charged over alleged indecent act at Penang cemetery
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: A couple arrested in connection with an alleged indecent act at a Chinese cemetery here will be charged at the magistrate's court tomorrow. Northeast district police chief Assistant Commissioner Abdul Rozak Muhammad said the two individuals, aged 37 and 58, will be charged under Section 377D of the Penal Code. "The couple, held in connection with the case, will be charged under Section 377D of the Penal Code at the George Town magistrate's court tomorrow," he said.

items.title: Police record 10 statements in Nilai cremation‑urn theft; four urns recovered
items.summary: SEREMBAN, March 25 — Police have so far recorded statements from 10 witnesses in connection with the theft of 30 cremation urns containing human ashes from a memorial park in Nilai, reported on February 26. Nilai police chief Supt Johari Yahya said four urns have been recovered to date, but full verification and a complete inventory check are still being carried out by the memorial park management. “The investigation is ongoing, and police are systematically recording witness statements, including from the memorial park staff, while examining all relevant angles of the case.

items.title: More than 3,800 foreign nationals checked in nationwide immigration sweep
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA – More than 3,800 foreign nationals were screened under Ops Pantau, a nationwide enforcement operation carried out by the Immigration Department over five days from March 20 to 24, according to Sinar Harian . Immigration Department Director-General Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said 45 Ops Pantau operations were conducted at identified locations, involving 3,826 individuals. However, every action is based on intelligence and enforcement needs, not timing alone,” Zakaria said.

items.title: Domestic Trade Ministry: Fisherman in viral Penang RON95 video had valid permit, no offence committed
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 25 — The Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) has confirmed that the man in a viral video shown filling a drum with RON95 at a Jalan Tengah petrol station in Bayan Lepas did not breach any laws. State director S Jegan said checks revealed that the individual is a licensed fisherman with a valid permit for fuel purchases, within the established quota limits. Jegan explained that any fuel purchase exceeding 20 litres in a drum requires a special permit for scheduled controlled goods from KPDN, typically issued for specific uses such as operating agricultural or fisheries machinery, and subject to the conditions set.

items.title: Police smash 'Harraz Gang', four men nabbed for vehicle thefts, burglaries
items.summary: MELAKA: Melaka police have successfully dismantled an organised crime group known as the "Harraz Gang", believed to be the mastermind behind a string of vehicle thefts, housebreaking and robberies in the Melaka Tengah district since the beginning of this year. Melaka Tengah district police chief, Assistant Commissioner Christopher Patit said the success followed the arrest of four local men in a special operation, 'Op Lejang Melaka' carried out by the D4 (Intelligence/Operations) Division of the Criminal Investigation Department (CID) in Permatang Pasir, Alai, on March 19. Christopher said the case is being investigated under Section 379A of the Penal Code for vehicle theft, Section 457 of the Penal Code for housebreaking and Section 392 of the Penal Code for robbery.

items.title: Globaltec's Australian unit flags no undisclosed info after shares double
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Globaltec Formation Bhd said its Australia-listed subsidiary, NuEnergy Gas Ltd, is not aware of any undisclosed information that could explain the surge in its share price and trading volume. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, Globaltec said NuEnergy had responded to a price query from the Australian Securities Exchange (ASX), confirming that it had complied with disclosure requirements and that no material information had been withheld from the market. In its response, NuEnergy pointed to positive independent analyst commentary published in Australian media as a possible factor behind the heightened investor interest.

items.title: MoE directs schools to step up preparedness for hot weather
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: All educational institutions under the Education Ministry (MoE) have been instructed to enhance preparedness and take appropriate measures to cope with hot weather when schools reopen on Sunday and Monday. The ministry said key measures include postponing outdoor activities if temperatures exceed 35 degrees Celsius and monitoring activities that may expose students to hot conditions to safeguard their health. School management may also consider allowing students, teachers and support staff to wear appropriate sports attire during hot weather, depending on prevailing conditions.

items.title: Pulau Sebatik declared drought disaster zone amid prolonged dry spell
items.summary: TAWAU, March 25 — The Tawau District Disaster Management Committee (JPBD) today declared a state of drought affecting Pulau Sebatik. Tawau Municipal Council president Datuk Joseph Pang, who also chairs the committee, said the declaration covers the island area within the district. “It is hereby declared at 10 am today that a drought disaster has occurred in Pulau Sebatik, Tawau,” he said in a statement.

items.title: MOE directs schools to boost preparedness for hot weather, postpone outdoor activities if temperatures exceed 35°C
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 25 — All educational institutions under the Ministry of Education (MOE) have been instructed to enhance preparedness and take appropriate measures to cope with hot weather when schools reopen this Monday (March 30). In a statement today, the MOE said key measures include postponing outdoor activities if temperatures exceed 35 degrees Celsius and monitoring activities that may expose students to hot conditions to safeguard their health. School management may also consider allowing students, teachers and support staff to wear appropriate sports attire during hot weather, depending on prevailing conditions.

items.title: MOH tells public to take precautions as Malaysia records more heat‑related illnesses amid prolonged hot weather
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 25 — The prolonged hot weather that is currently affecting the country can be detrimental to human health with a spectrum of heat-related illnesses that can range from mild to severe and potentially fatal, according to the Ministry of Health (MOH). “All individuals exposed to hot weather for a long period of time are at risk of experiencing heat-related illnesses, but children, the elderly, individuals with chronic diseases, workers exposed to hot weather, and individuals who do physical activities during hot weather are at high risk,” according to the statement. MOH announced that based on continuous monitoring from January 1 to March 24, a total of 15 cases of heat-related illnesses nationwide were recorded, involving 11 cases of heat exhaustion, one case of heat cramps, and three cases of heat stroke.

items.title: SAR halted for missing Pulau Tenggol diver after four days as no new leads emerge
items.summary: DUNGUN, March 25 — The search and rescue operation (SAR) for a diver who was reported missing in the waters of Pulau Tenggol here since March 21 was ended yesterday evening. Kemaman Maritime Zone director, Maritime Commander Abdul Halim Hamzah said the search operation for the victim, Tam Peng Hoong, 66, was ended after no new leads were found. He said the operation involved marine assets from the Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency and assistance from the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) and two diving centres.

items.title: MetMalaysia places Pokok Sena, Pendang and Baling under Level 2 heat‑wave alert, with 16 other areas at Level 1
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — Three districts in Kedah are currently recording a Level 2 heat wave, while 16 other areas across Peninsular Malaysia are at Level 1 which is on alert as of this morning. Meanwhile, the 16 areas placed under Level 1 are Sik, Padang Terap, Kubang Pasu, Langkawi, Kuala Muda, Kulim and Bandar Baharu in Kedah; the entire Perlis as well as the North East and North areas in Penang. Meanwhile, Level 1 status refers to daily maximum temperatures reaching 35 to 37 degrees Celsius for at least three consecutive days.

items.title: Malaysia’s private hospitals report no disruption to medicine supply despite West Asia war, says APHM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — Medicine supply in private hospitals across the country remains stable amid the war in West Asia, the Association of Private Hospitals Malaysia (APHM) said today. The association said the country’s pharmaceutical supply chain has not reported any indications of being directly affected by the ongoing conflict. However, the association said it will closely monitor the developments in the region and the subsequent impact on the cost of medicines and medical consumables.

items.title: On Police Day, IGP says PDRM’s 219-year legacy shows unwavering commitment to national security
items.summary: =KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) remains a steadfast bulwark in preserving national security and stability, with more than two centuries of history and sacrifice. Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said that behind the blue uniform worn by officers lie countless stories of courage, sacrifice and selflessness, given without expectation of reward. In the same post, Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay, PERKEP Malaysia chairman Datin Seri Datuk Dr Munirah Che Rose, and vice-chairman Datuk Zalena Mohd Ariffin also conveyed their appreciation in conjunction with Police Day celebrations.

items.title: Seven-year-old boy dies after car hits him while crossing road in Kota Kuala Muda
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, March 25 — A seven-year-old boy was killed after being hit by a car while crossing the road along Jalan Kota Kuala Muda near here last night. Muhammad Ammar Zamri, a pupil of Sekolah Kebangsaan Kota Kuala Muda, was believed to have suddenly crossed the road before being struck by a Perodua Myvi. Kuala Muda police chief ACP Hanyan Ramlan said the incident occurred at 9.23pm, with police receiving a report at 9.45pm.

items.title: From ‘agak‑agak’ to ‘Malaysia boleh’: Oxford English Dictionary adds new Malaysia and Singapore words in latest update
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — The Oxford English Dictionary (OED) has added several Malaysian and Singaporean English terms in its latest update, highlighting the region’s linguistic influence on global English. Oxford Languages announced the additions in a press release yesterday, noting that many of the new entries originate from Malay and Hokkien. BTO , n. BTO , v. degazette , v. ice kacang , n. jialat , adj.

items.title: Saifuddin Nasution commends police for being ever-ready to safeguard the country
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — The role of a police officer is more than just a profession; it is a solemn responsibility to the nation, said Minister of Home Affairs Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil also extended his heartfelt greetings, expressing his appreciation for the police, particularly as they continue to carry out their duties. Happy Police Day!” he wrote on Facebook.

items.title: MMEA rescues five Thai sailors from burning cargo ship off Kota Tinggi
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, March 25 — The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) rescued five Thai crew members after their cargo ship caught fire about 30 nautical miles east of Tanjung Sedili here yesterday. MMEA Tanjung Sedili maritime zone director Maritime Commander Mohd Najib Sam said the Johor Bahru Maritime Rescue Sub Centre (MRSC) received a distress call in the incident at 12.20pm. “All five male crew members, aged between 25 and 61, were rescued via a medical evacuation (medevac) operation.

items.title: Trial run of Jalan Genting Highlands road charge system to start April 2, no payment required
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — Lingkaran Cekap Sdn Bhd (LCSB), the authorised operator for Jalan Genting Highlands, will begin a trial run of its road charge system on April 2, ahead of full implementation. The test will be conducted at two locations: the Genting Sempah entry to Genting Highlands and the Gohtong Jaya entry at the Gohtong Jaya roundabout. Late last year, Genting Malaysia confirmed plans to introduce a private vehicle charge on Jalan Genting Highlands based on a “user pays” principle to support ongoing maintenance and emergency response needs.

items.title: Hot weather in Malaysia likely to persist until June, warns MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — The hot weather phenomenon currently affecting the country is expected to continue until the beginning of the Southwest Monsoon (MBD) in June, following drier atmospheric conditions and lower rainfall, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). MetMalaysia deputy director-general (Operations) Ambun Dindang said Malaysia is currently in the final phase of the Northeast Monsoon (MTL) which is usually characterised by reduced rainfall and hotter and drier weather conditions, especially in the northern and interior areas of the Peninsula. He said based on local and international weather model projections, the country is expected to experience drier weather patterns with lower rainfall than usual for the next few months.
